title: Fresh Pasta Dough Ingredient Scaler
explanation: Adjust the number of doughs to see how ingredient amounts scale.

title: Fresh Pasta Recipe Scaler
explanation: Adjust serving size and ingredient amounts to see how the pasta dough scales.

cells:
servings = int(slider_servings)
eggs = int(slider_eggs)
flour_cups = float(slider_flour_cups)
scaling = servings / 2.0
eggs_needed = eggs * scaling
flour_needed_cups = flour_cups * scaling
total_time_minutes = servings * 15
print(f"Servings: {servings}")
print(f"Eggs needed: {int(eggs_needed)}")
print(f"Flour (cups): {flour_needed_cups:.2f}")
print(f"Estimated total time (minutes): {total_time_minutes}")

title: Pasta dough ingredient and time calculator
explanation: Adjusting ingredient amounts and cooking time based on the number of servings.

title: Italian double zero flour dough recipe scaler
explanation: Adjust sliders to see how ingredient amounts change with servings and egg usage.

title: Recipe scaler for egg dough
explanation: Adjusting servings and flour per egg lets you scale the dough proportionally.

title: Recipe Scaler for Pasta Dough
explanation: Adjusting ingredient quantities lets you scale the dough recipe for any number of servings.

cells:
# Compute scaled ingredient amounts based on servings
 total_eggs = servings
 total_flour_cups = servings * 0.5
 total_salt_tsp = servings * 0.25

 print(f"Eggs needed: {total_eggs}")
 print(f"Flour (cups): {total_flour_cups:.2f}")
 print(f"Salt (tsp): {total_salt_tsp:.2f}")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
 labels = ['Eggs', 'Flour (cups)', 'Salt (tsp)']
 values = [total_eggs, total_flour_cups, total_salt_tsp]
 plt.bar(labels, values)
 plt.ylabel('Amount')
 plt.title('Scaled Ingredient Quantities')
 plt.show()

title: Recipe scaling – cooking time vs servings
explanation: The viewer can see how total preparation time changes as the number of servings varies.
